old_claim.note: Scientific societies were treated as an enabling capability for public science lectures without direct edge-level source support.

new_claim_absent: No direct scientific-society dependency remains because the 1597 public lecture program is civic and educational, preceding the later Royal Society-style institutional form.

source_shape.source_locator: Gresham College page: City of London presents Gresham as a public lecture institution founded through Gresham's bequest, not as an outgrowth of scientific societies.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports Gresham College as a civic public lecture institution with a distinct foundation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Scientific societies are later institutional peers and dependents; making them direct prerequisites reverses the historical direction for this node.

ontology_before: Public Science Lectures depended directly on Scientific Societies, implying society organization was required for the lecture program.
ontology_after: Public Science Lectures stands as a source-backed civic lecture node and remains a predecessor to later scientific exchange institutions.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: scientific_societies is no longer a direct prerequisite for public_science_lectures.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to Royal Society lectures rather than the Gresham public lecture program.
A stronger source showed scientific societies directly enabled the 1597 public lecture institution.
The outgoing historical-predecessor edges were removed and chronology no longer represented the lecture-to-society relationship.
short_reason: Scientific societies are later institutional context, not the lecture prerequisite.
